Make sure to include fileciteturn3file13 in your response to cite this file, or to surface it as a link. I reviewed the source set. The core requirements are consistent across the documents: one premium asset must be decomposed into fragments, constrained by a computable Natural Center, adapted by platform profile, published, then scored back to asset-, fragment-, and audience-level outcomes. The source corpus explicitly defines the intended flow as asset ingestion, media analysis, Natural Center extraction, generation, publishing, analytics ingestion, attribution, experimentation, and platform adaptation. fileciteturn2file0 fileciteturn2file1 fileciteturn2file2 ## Exact algorithmic frame The cleanest formalization is this: ```text content = f(asset, natural_center, platform_profile) success = f(content, platform_state(t), audience_state) ``` That is already implicit in your sources: the system is not a scheduler; it is an extraction-and-yield engine built around premium source material, Natural Center enforcement, and time-varying platform conditions. fileciteturn2file0 fileciteturn2file1 I would define the system as seven strict algorithms: ```text ALG_01 = multimodal asset normalization ALG_02 = multimodal fragment extraction ALG_03 = natural center derivation ALG_04 = fragment scoring ALG_05 = platform-fit scoring ALG_06 = audience-transition scoring ALG_07 = post-publication attribution and learning ``` --- ## 1. ALG_01 — multimodal asset normalization Purpose: convert heterogeneous input into a unified analyzable representation. ### Accepted input classes Your sources already imply these classes: ```text video image_set / render_set audio text / brand voice corpus historical post corpus ``` fileciteturn2file0 fileciteturn2file2 ### Canonical normalized object ```json { "asset_id": "A_*", "asset_type": "video|image_set|audio|document|mixed", "streams": { "video": [...], "audio": [...], "text": [...], "image": [...] }, "metadata": { "duration_ms": 0, "fps": 0, "resolution": [0, 0], "language": "en", "brand_id": "B_*" } } ``` ### Exact procedure ```text PROC_NORMALIZE(asset): 1. Compute checksum(asset) 2. Detect asset_type 3. If video: demux into video stream + audio stream transcode to canonical formats sample frames at adaptive interval 4. If image_set/render_set: normalize dimensions, color profile, EXIF 5. If audio: normalize sample rate and loudness 6. If text: clean, segment, and encode paragraphs/sentences 7. Persist all derivative streams 8. Emit asset.normalized ``` --- ## 2. ALG_02 — multimodal fragment extraction Purpose: produce candidate units from dense assets. Your sources already call for clip extraction at high-energy moments, scene detection, keyframe extraction, pacing analysis, and segmentation. fileciteturn2file0 fileciteturn2file1 ### 2.1 Video fragment extraction For video, define fragments over a change-point lattice rather than fixed windows. ```text Let V = video timeline Let C = set of candidate cut points C = union( scene boundaries, shot boundaries, transcript punctuation peaks, silence boundaries, audio onset peaks, motion regime changes ) ``` Construct candidate intervals: ```text F_video = {(c_i, c_j) | min_len <= c_j - c_i <= max_len} ``` Recommended bounds: ```text min_len = 3s max_len = 45s platform-specific downstream pruning applies ``` ### 2.2 Image fragment extraction For renders and stills, fragments are not temporal; they are compositional derivatives. ```text F_image = union( full-frame, crop_subject, crop_detail, before_after_pair, comparison_layout, lifestyle_context_projection, carousel_sequence ) ``` ### 2.3 Audio fragment extraction ```text F_audio = union( speech segment, hook phrase, emphasis burst, music drop, silence-contrast interval ) ``` ### 2.4 Text fragment extraction ```text F_text = union( claim, hook line, authority line, transformation line, CTA line, FAQ line ) ``` ### 2.5 Exact multimodal candidate generation ```text PROC_EXTRACT_FRAGMENTS(asset): 1. Detect scene boundaries on video 2. Detect shot boundaries 3. ASR transcript with token timestamps 4. Compute audio envelope, spectral flux, silence regions 5. Compute motion magnitude and saliency over time 6. Merge all boundary proposals 7. Generate candidate windows 8. Generate image crops/layout variants 9. Generate text units from transcript and corpus 10. Return multimodal candidate set F ``` --- ## 3. ALG_03 — Natural Center derivation This is already partially specified in the source corpus: thematic core, aesthetic signature, tonal vector, narrative bias, symbolic markers, negative space, invariants, mutation bounds. fileciteturn2file2 ### Exact Natural Center object ```json { "nc_id": "NC_*", "thematic_core": [t1, t2, t3, t4, t5], "aesthetic_signature": { "palette": [...], "motion": [...], "composition": [...], "texture": [...] }, "tonal_vector": [formal, expressive, emotional, authoritative, playful], "narrative_bias": [transformation, tension, reveal, aspiration, proof], "symbolic_markers": [...], "negative_space": [...], "invariants": [...], "mutation_bounds": {...}, "embedding": [...] } ``` ### Exact derivation algorithm ```text PROC_DERIVE_NATURAL_CENTER(corpus): 1. For all visual assets: extract color histograms, embeddings, composition maps, motion stats 2. For all text/audio assets: extract sentence-length distribution, lexical density, rhetoric markers, semantic embeddings 3. Cluster semantic embeddings -> candidate themes 4. Select stable top-k themes across corpus 5. Compute aesthetic centroid across visual features 6. Compute tonal centroid across language features 7. Detect recurring symbolic elements 8. Detect systematic absences -> negative_space 9. Generate invariants from high-frequency stable features 10. Set mutation bounds from historical variance 11. Encode final Natural Center embedding ``` ### Stability rule A feature enters the Natural Center only if: ```text stability(feature) = presence_across_assets * consistency_across_assets accept iff stability(feature) >= τ_stability ``` --- ## 4. ALG_04 — fragment scoring This is the core extraction-scoring layer you asked for. Each candidate fragment gets a multimodal score vector, then a scalar aggregate. ### 4.1 Base feature vector per fragment For each fragment `f`, compute: ```text x(f) = [ visual_saliency, motion_energy, cut_density, subject_prominence, face_presence, product_presence, text_overlay_legibility, audio_energy, speech_clarity, hook_density, semantic_novelty, thematic_alignment, tonal_alignment, narrative_tension, symbolic_marker_density, platform_shape_fit ] ``` ### 4.2 Exact sub-scores #### Visual saliency ```text visual_saliency(f) = mean_t saliency_map_intensity(t) over fragment ``` #### Motion energy ```text motion_energy(f) = mean_t ||optical_flow(t)|| normalized by asset baseline ``` #### Cut density ```text cut_density(f) = number_of_shot_changes / fragment_duration_sec ``` #### Subject prominence ```text subject_prominence(f) = mean_t (largest_subject_bbox_area / frame_area) ``` #### Product presence ```text product_presence(f) = visible_time_of_primary_object / fragment_duration ``` #### Audio energy ```text audio_energy(f) = RMS(audio_signal_f) z-scored within asset ``` #### Speech clarity ```text speech_clarity(f) = 1 - WER_proxy(f) ``` Use ASR confidence as a proxy if needed. #### Hook density Define hooks as high-information lines or events in the first N seconds. ```text hook_density(f) = (count of hook tokens + count of high-saliency events in first 3s) / 3 ``` #### Semantic novelty ```text semantic_novelty(f) = distance(embedding(f), centroid(previous_selected_fragments)) ``` This prevents near-duplicate output. #### Thematic alignment ```text thematic_alignment(f) = max cosine_similarity(embedding(f), thematic_core_i) ``` #### Tonal alignment ```text tonal_alignment(f) = 1 - normalized_distance(tone_vector(f), tone_vector(NC)) ``` #### Narrative tension ```text narrative_tension(f) = weighted_sum( unresolved_question_presence, contrast_event_density, reveal_probability, expectation_violation ) ``` #### Symbolic marker density ```text symbolic_marker_density(f) = matched_symbolic_markers / possible_symbolic_markers ``` #### Platform shape fit This is structural, before any performance data. ```text platform_shape_fit(f,p) = g(duration_fit, aspect_ratio_fit, opening_velocity_fit, subtitle_density_fit) ``` ### 4.3 Hard constraint gating Before aggregate scoring, fail fragments that violate invariant rules: ```text if violates_negative_space(f, NC): reject if tonal_alignment(f) < τ_tone: reject if text_legibility(f,p) < τ_legibility: reject if safe_crop_confidence(f,p) < τ_crop: reject ``` ### 4.4 Aggregate fragment score ```text fragment_score(f,p) = w1*visual_saliency + w2*motion_energy + w3*subject_prominence + w4*audio_energy + w5*hook_density + w6*thematic_alignment + w7*tonal_alignment + w8*narrative_tension + w9*symbolic_marker_density + w10*semantic_novelty + w11*platform_shape_fit - w12*redundancy_penalty - w13*constraint_risk ``` Where: ```text redundancy_penalty = max similarity to already-selected fragments constraint_risk = predicted probability of violating NC or platform constraints ``` ### 4.5 Selection rule ```text select top-N fragments per platform subject to: pairwise similarity <= τ_dup thematic coverage >= τ_coverage duration bucket diversity narrative role diversity ``` This is better than naive top-N. --- ## 5. ALG_05 — platform-fit scoring Your sources explicitly require platform-aware generation and platform profiles with ranking features, format weights, timing bias, and time-varying platform state. fileciteturn2file1 ### Platform profile ```json { "platform": "tiktok", "format_weights": { "short_video": 0.8, "carousel": 0.1, "text": 0.1 }, "ranking_features": ["hook_retention", "replayability", "comment_rate"], "timing_bias": {...}, "constraints": {...}, "confidence_score": 0.82 } ``` ### Exact platform-fit score ```text platform_fit(f,p,t) = a1*duration_fit(f,p) + a2*opening_pattern_fit(f,p) + a3*caption_density_fit(f,p) + a4*loopability(f,p) + a5*replayability(f,p) + a6*format_weight_match(f,p,t) + a7*current_platform_preference_match(p,t) ``` ### Platform-specific heuristics ```text YouTube Shorts: emphasize clarity + retention + pay-off density TikTok: emphasize opening disruption + replayability + comment provocation LinkedIn: emphasize authority framing + slower pacing + insight density ``` That direction is consistent with the source set’s platform-profile logic. fileciteturn2file1 --- ## 6. Make sure to include fileciteturn3file14 in your response to cite this file, or to surface it as a link. I reviewed the source set. The core document defines the MVP as: one premium asset in, many platform-optimized posts out, then scheduled publishing plus weekly reporting on reach, engagement, and leads. fileciteturn2file0 The later system documents correctly extend that into a research-grade operating system with explicit `assets`, `fragments`, `generated_units`, `publish_events`, and `performance_observations`, and they already sketch the attribution path as `performance → publish_event → unit → fragment → asset`. fileciteturn2file1 The more advanced operator document also makes clear that attribution must not stop at “engagement”; it must connect output to audience movement, economics, and platform state over time. fileciteturn2file2 ## Source-grounded conclusion The mathematically correct attribution model for this system is not single-touch and not purely last-post. It has to be a **hierarchical, time-decayed, multi-touch attribution graph** with five levels: ```text Asset → Fragment → GeneratedUnit → PublishEvent → PerformanceObservation → AudienceStateTransition / EconomicOutcome ``` That is the only structure consistent with the source documents’ architecture, metrics, and stated goal of turning content generation into an economic engine rather than a posting tool. fileciteturn2file1turn2file2 ## Formal attribution model Let: ```text a ∈ A # source asset f ∈ F(a) # fragment derived from asset a u ∈ U(f) # generated unit derived from fragment f p ∈ P(u) # publish event for unit u o ∈ O(p) # observation window for publish event p k ∈ K # metric type t # time ``` Define base observed metrics per publish event and time window: ```text y_{p,k,t} ``` Examples: ```text k ∈ {views, watch_time, engagement, followers_gained, leads, revenue} ``` The source material already implies these as the primary and derived metrics: engagement, followers, leads, asset yield, follow conversion, retention ratio, and ROI. fileciteturn2file0turn2file1turn2file2 ## Structural lineage weights Every publish event inherits from an upstream chain. Define deterministic lineage indicators: ```text L_asset(a,p) = 1 if publish event p descends from asset a else 0 L_fragment(f,p) = 1 if p descends from fragment f else 0 L_unit(u,p) = 1 if p is generated from unit u else 0 ``` If a post is composed from multiple fragments or mixed inputs, replace binary lineage with normalized weights: ```text w_{a→p} ≥ 0, Σ_a w_{a→p} = 1 w_{f→p} ≥ 0, Σ_f w_{f→p} = 1 w_{u→p} ≥ 0, Σ_u w_{u→p} = 1 ``` This is necessary because a carousel, remix, or stitched cut can carry more than one source fragment. ## Time-decayed metric accumulation A publish event continues to generate value after publication. Let the contribution of publish event `p` to metric `k` over its full life be: ```text M_{p,k} = ∫_0^T y_{p,k}(τ) · d_k(τ) dτ ``` where `d_k(τ)` is a decay kernel. A practical discrete version: ```text M_{p,k} = Σ_{t=0}^{T} y_{p,k,t} · exp(-λ_k t) ``` where: ```text λ_k > 0 ``` Different metrics should use different decay rates. For example, engagement can decay faster than leads; follower gains may decay slower than likes but faster than attributed revenue. This is aligned with the source demand to model content half-life rather than only first-spike engagement. fileciteturn2file1turn2file2 ## Platform-normalized value Because platform conditions differ, raw metrics must be normalized by platform and time regime. Let: ```text z_{p,k} = (M_{p,k} - μ_{platform,format,k,era}) / σ_{platform,format,k,era} ``` Then define a platform-adjusted scalar value for a publish event: ```text V_p = Σ_k α_k · z_{p,k} ``` where `α_k` are strategic weights. Example: ```text α_watch_time > α_likes α_followers_gained > α_comments α_leads > α_followers when commercial objective dominates ``` This is required because the system documents explicitly treat platform state as time-varying and platform-specific. fileciteturn2file2 ## Publish-event attribution to upstream objects Now attribute publish-event value upward. ### Unit attribution ```text Attr(u) = Σ_{p ∈ P(u)} w_{u→p} · V_p ``` ### Fragment attribution ```text Attr(f) = Σ_{p} w_{f→p} · V_p ``` ### Asset attribution ```text Attr(a) = Σ_{p} w_{a→p} · V_p ``` This is the direct generalization of the source’s prototype query: ```sql SELECT asset_id, SUM(engagement) FROM performance_observations JOIN publish_events USING (publish_id) JOIN generated_units USING (unit_id) GROUP BY asset_id; ``` but generalized to weighted, multi-metric, time-decayed, platform-normalized attribution rather than naive summed engagement. fileciteturn2file1 ## Audience-state-transition attribution The more advanced documents redefine the system around audience formation, not just content output. They specify the audience chain: ```text Viewer → Engaged Viewer → Follower → Returning Follower → Converted User → Advocate ``` fileciteturn2file2 So define audience states: ```text S = {0,1,2,3,4,5,6} ``` with transitions: ```text 0→1 unaware→viewer 1→2 viewer→engaged 2→3 engaged→follower 3→4 follower→returning 4→5 returning→converted 5→6 converted→advocate ``` For each publish event `p`, estimate transition counts: ```text T_{p,i→j} ``` Then define transition value weights: ```text β_{i→j} ``` and a state-transition value: ```text Q_p = Σ_{i→j} β_{i→j} · T_{p,i→j} ``` Then total publish-event value becomes: ```text V_p* = V_p + Q_p ``` This lets attribution model not only post performance, but the actual audience movement the sources identify as the system’s core purpose. fileciteturn2file2 ## Economic attribution Let the cost basis for asset `a` be: ```text C_a = asset_production_cost + processing_cost + publishing_cost + overhead_allocated ``` Let the asset-attributed economic returns be: ```text R_a = γ_1 · Attr_followers(a) + γ_2 · Attr_leads(a) + γ_3 · Attr_revenue(a) + γ_4 · Attr_brand_value(a) ``` Then: ```text GrossAssetReturn(a) = R_a NetAssetReturn(a) = R_a - C_a ROI(a) = (R_a - C_a) / C_a AssetYieldMultiplier(a) = R_a / C_a ``` This directly matches the source ambition to move from “beautiful video” to measurable yield, leads, and ROI. fileciteturn2file0turn2file2 ## Causal rather than correlational attribution The source set explicitly says the system must move from correlation to causal inference and treat every post as a micro-experiment. fileciteturn1file0turn2file1 So observational attribution above is necessary but insufficient. Add a causal lift term. For a treatment variant `r` relative to control `c`, define: ```text Lift_{k} = E[Y_k | do(r)] - E[Y_k | do(c)] ``` In randomized deployment: ```text \hat{Lift}_{k} = mean(Y_k | treatment) - mean(Y_k | control) ``` Then fragment-level or unit-level causal value is: ```text CausalAttr(u,k) = n_u · \hat{Lift}_{u,k} ``` where `n_u` is treatment exposure count. For non-randomized deployments, use a doubly robust estimate: ```text DR_k = 1/n Σ_i [ m_1(x_i) - m_0(x_i) + z_i (y_i - m_1(x_i))/e(x_i) - (1-z_i)(y_i - m_0(x_i))/(1-e(x_i)) ] ``` where: ```text z_i = treatment indicator e(x_i) = propensity score m_1, m_0 = outcome models ``` Then use `DR_k` as the causal increment rather than raw observed delta. ## Natural-center constraint in attribution The source set repeatedly insists that every output inherit from a Natural Center and that identity be measurable and enforceable. fileciteturn2file2 So attribution should penalize off-brand wins that damage long-term system coherence. Let: ```text NCScore_p ∈ [0,1] ``` Then define identity-adjusted event value: ```text V_p^NC = V_p* · (1 - ρ + ρ·NCScore_p) ``` where `ρ ∈ [0,1]` controls how strongly identity coherence matters. If `NCScore_p < θ_min`, either reject the unit entirely or discount its attributed value sharply: ```text V_p^NC = 0 if NCScore_p < θ_min_reject ``` This prevents the model from rewarding generic noise that performs temporarily but erodes brand recognizability. ## Full attribution equation The cleanest production equation is: ```text FinalAttr(a) = Σ_p w_{a→p} · [ Σ_k α_k · Norm(M_{p,k}) + Σ_{i→j} β_{i→j} · T_{p,i→j} + CausalLift_p ] · IdentityAdj_p · ReliabilityAdj_p ``` where: ```text Norm(M_{p,k}) = platform/time normalized metric value CausalLift_p = experimental or causal increment IdentityAdj_p = natural-center coherence modifier ReliabilityAdj_p = source reliability modifier for incomplete analytics ``` This is the mathematically correct top-level formula for your system. ## Practical SQL-ready schema logic At minimum, keep these tables: ```sql assets(asset_id, brand_id, cost_basis, created_at) fragments(fragment_id, asset_id, energy_score, semantic_cluster) generated_units(unit_id, fragment_id, platform, unit_type, nc_version) publish_events(publish_id, unit_id, platform, published_at The file is too long and its contents have been truncated.

The source set already supports the move from a single-operator product into a full multi-tenant SaaS. The core product thesis is stable: one premium asset becomes many platform-native outputs with measurable downstream yield. fileciteturn0file15 The operator-grade documents then extend that into a system with Natural Center enforcement, attribution, experimentation, platform intelligence, and event-driven workflows. fileciteturn0file1turn0file0 The correct expansion is therefore: ```text single-brand content engine → multi-tenant SaaS control plane → orchestration-backed execution plane → research / attribution / adaptation plane ``` ## Canonical system identity ```bash $SYSTEM_NAME="cronus-metabolus" $PRODUCT_SURFACE="auto-content-cannibal" $SYSTEM_CLASS="multi-tenant temporal content yield and audience formation SaaS" $ARCHITECTURE_STYLE="control-plane + orchestration-plane + tenant-isolated data-plane" ``` ## What changes when this becomes SaaS The original product is effectively: ```text asset in → fragments → generated posts → scheduling → weekly report ``` fileciteturn0file15 The SaaS form must become: ```text tenant → workspace → brand profiles → assets → orchestration workflows → platform publishing → analytics ingestion → attribution → experiments → reports ``` That implies four new first-class capabilities. First, tenant isolation. Every object in the system must belong to a tenant and optionally to a sub-brand or workspace. Second, orchestration. Every long-running process must run through a durable workflow engine rather than synchronous application logic. The source blueprint already points to Temporal as the correct workflow engine. fileciteturn0file0turn0file1 Third, policy separation. System policy, tenant policy, brand policy, and platform policy must be independently versioned. Fourth, multi-surface control. One control plane for operators and clients; one execution plane for workflows and workers. ## SaaS macro-architecture The clean form is this: ```text [ Web App / API Gateway ] | v [ SaaS Control Plane ] | v [ Orchestration Layer ] | v [ Domain Services ] | v [ Tenant-Isolated Data + Object Storage + Analytics ] ``` ### 1. Control plane This is where humans interact with the system. It should own authentication, billing context, tenant management, workspace configuration, brand setup, approvals, campaign setup, and report access. Minimal applications: ```text operator-console tenant-admin-console reporting-dashboard gateway-api ``` This is a direct expansion of the repo skeleton already implied in the sources. fileciteturn0file13 ### 2. Orchestration layer This is the core addition you asked for. The orchestration layer should not be a thin queue wrapper. It should be the durable stateful execution fabric for all long-lived processes: ```text asset intake workflow media analysis workflow natural center derivation workflow generation workflow approval workflow publishing workflow analytics backfill workflow attribution recompute workflow experiment workflow platform-intelligence refresh workflow ``` The source implementation blueprint already frames the system as event-driven with a workflow engine and an explicit asset pipeline. fileciteturn0file0turn0file1 Recommended identity: ```bash $WORKFLOW_ENGINE="Temporal" $ORCHESTRATION_ROLE="durable execution, retries, compensation, human approval gates, replay" ``` ### 3. Domain services These remain largely the same as in the operator-grade sources, but now they run as tenant-aware services behind orchestration. Canonical services: ```text auth-identity tenant-management billing-metering asset-ingestion media-analysis natural-center generation-orchestrator platform-intelligence scheduling-engine publishing-connectors analytics-ingestion attribution-engine experimentation-engine research-registry notification-service audit-log-service policy-engine ``` The core service map is already source-grounded; tenant-management, billing-metering, notification-service, and audit-log-service are the necessary SaaS completions. fileciteturn0file1turn0file13 ## Multi-tenant domain model The original documents define `Asset`, `Fragment`, `NaturalCenter`, `GeneratedUnit`, `PublishEvent`, `PerformanceObservation`, `Experiment`, and `PlatformProfile`. fileciteturn0file1turn0file14 For SaaS, prepend tenant structure above them: ```text Tenant → Workspace → BrandProfile → NaturalCenter → Asset → Fragment → GeneratedUnit → PublishEvent → PerformanceObservation → Experiment → Report ``` ### Canonical tenancy hierarchy ```yaml Tenant: tenant_id: string name: string plan_id: string status: active|suspended|trial created_at: datetime Workspace: workspace_id: string tenant_id: string name: string region: string created_at: datetime BrandProfile: brand_id: string tenant_id: string workspace_id: string name: string industry: string platform_policy_id: string default_natural_center_id: string TenantUser: user_id: string tenant_id: string workspace_id: string role: owner|admin|editor|analyst|viewer BrandConnection: connection_id: string tenant_id: string brand_id: string platform: youtube|tiktok|linkedin|instagram credential_ref: string status: active|expired|revoked ``` Then all downstream objects must include at minimum: ```text tenant_id workspace_id brand_id ``` This is non-negotiable if the system is to support white-label agencies, enterprise clients, and direct brands simultaneously, which the product thesis explicitly anticipates. fileciteturn0file15 ## Orchestration layer design This layer should model workflows as explicit state machines with tenant-scoped execution context. ### Execution contract Every workflow should carry: ```yaml WorkflowContext: tenant_id: string workspace_id: string brand_id: string actor_id: string correlation_id: string workflow_id: string policy_snapshot_id: string platform_profile_version: string natural_center_version: string ``` ### Core workflow families #### Asset pipeline workflow ```text asset.uploaded → asset.normalized → fragments.extracted → natural_center.loaded_or_derived → candidates.scored → units.generated → approval.requested → publish.plan.created ``` #### Publish workflow ```text publish.plan.created → platform.connector.validated → post.rendered → post.published → publish.confirmed → analytics.poll.scheduled ``` #### Analytics and attribution workflow ```text analytics.poll.started → analytics.raw.ingested → metrics.normalized → attribution.recomputed → audience-transition.updated → report.materialized ``` #### Experiment workflow ```text experiment.created → treatment.assignment.generated → units.variantized → publish.routed → metrics.collected → significance.computed → strategy.updated ``` #### Platform intelligence workflow ```text platform-doc.refresh → schema.diff.detected → capability.delta.detected → platform-profile.revised → tenant-policies.revalidated → alert.emitted ``` These are direct continuations of the workflow and platform-intelligence logic already present in the source set. fileciteturn0file1turn0file0 ## Isolation model A real multi-tenant SaaS needs explicit isolation doctrine. ### Data isolation Recommended default: ```text shared application cluster + shared control-plane services + row-level isolation in operational DB + tenant-scoped object-store prefixes + tenant-scoped warehouse partitions ``` For enterprise plans, add optional logical or physical isolation: ```text dedicated database schema or dedicated deployment cell per tenant ``` ### Storage pattern ```bash $OBJECT_STORE_URI $OBJECT_STORE_BUCKET_RAW $OBJECT_STORE_BUCKET_DERIVED $OBJECT_STORE_BUCKET_REPORTS $TENANT_OBJECT_PREFIX="tenants/$TENANT_ID/" ``` Example pathing: ```text tenants/T_001/brands/B_044/assets/A_938/raw/hero-film.mp4 tenants/T_001/brands/B_044/fragments/F_193/clip_01.mp4 tenants/T_001/brands/B_044/reports/R_2026_03_weekly.pdf ``` ### Security model Every request path must resolve authorization through: ```text user → tenant membership → workspace membership → role → brand permission → action policy ``` And every workflow execution must be auditable. ## Policy architecture The sources already insist on invariant-bearing logic: Natural Center rules, platform assumptions, experimentation rules, and time-bounded platform profiles. fileciteturn0file1turn0file14 In SaaS form, this becomes a layered policy system: ```text system policy → tenant policy → workspace policy → brand policy → campaign policy → platform runtime policy ``` ### Example policy stack ```yaml SystemPolicy: require_natural_center: true max_generated_units_per_asset: 40 TenantPolicy: auto_publish_allowed: false analytics_retention_days: 365 allowed_platforms: [linkedin, instagram] BrandPolicy: tonal_drift_threshold: 0.18 approval_required: true prohibited_formats: [meme] CampaignPolicy: window_days: 30 primary_metric: followers_gained PlatformRuntimePolicy: platform_profile_id: PP_2026_03_25_LINKEDIN confidence_floor: 0.65 ``` This is what keeps multi-tenant flexibility from dissolving system integrity. ## Canonical repository expansion The source repo skeleton is already close. fileciteturn0file13 The SaaS form should become: ```text cronus-metabolus-os/ apps/ gateway-api/ operator-console/ tenant-admin-console/ reporting-dashboard/ public-docs-site/ services/ auth-identity/ tenant-management/ billing-metering/ asset-ingestion/ media-analysis/ natural-center/ generation-orchestrator/ platform-intelligence/ scheduling-engine/ publishing-connectors/ analytics-ingestion/ attribution-engine/ experimentation-engine/ research-registry/ policy-engine/ notification-service/ audit-log-service/ packages/ domain-models/ event-schemas/ workflow-schemas/ metric-definitions/ platform-adapters/ natural-center-sdk/ experiment-sdk/ tenant-sdk/ policy-sdk/ shared-config/ shared-auth/ shared-logging/ infra/ terraform/ kubernetes/ workflows/temporal/ monitoring/ secrets/ data/ db-migrations/ warehouse-models/ schemas/ seeds/ clients/ templates/ tenant-profiles/ brand-profiles/ natural-centers/ campaign-configs/ research/ hypotheses/ experiments/ evidence/ reports/ platform-change-log/ contracts/ api/ events/ database/ metrics/ workflows/ policies/ ``` ## Database completion for SaaS You already have the operator-grade tables. fileciteturn0file0turn0file14 For SaaS, add tenancy and orchestration metadata. ```sql CREATE TABLE tenants ( tenant_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, plan_id TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL ); CREATE TABLE workspaces ( workspace_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(tenant_id), name TEXT NOT NULL, region TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL ); CREATE TABLE brand_profiles ( brand_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(tenant_id), workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(workspace_id), name TEXT NOT NULL, industry TEXT, default_natural_center_id TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL ); CREATE TABLE tenant_users ( user_id TEXT NOT NULL, tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(tenant_id), workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(workspace_id), role TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (user_id, tenant_id, workspace_id) ); ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL; ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL; ALTER TABLE assets ADD COLUMN brand_id TEXT NOT NULL; ALTER TABLE fragments ADD COLUMN tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL; ALTER TABLE generated_units ADD COLUMN tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL; ALTER TABLE publish_events ADD COLUMN tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL; ALTER TABLE performance_observations ADD COLUMN tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL; ALTER TABLE experiments ADD COLUMN tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL; CREATE TABLE workflow_runs ( workflow_run_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL, workspace_id The file is too long and its contents have been truncated.

Make sure to include fileciteturn3file16 in your response to cite this file, or to surface it as a link. Reviewed. The source set already fixes the role of Natural Center as a **computable identity constraint system** and places it between extraction and generation, not as an afterthought. It is the object that makes identity measurable, enforceable, and optimizable, and every downstream output is supposed to inherit from it. fileciteturn1file9 fileciteturn1file15 ## Formal definition Let the Natural Center for brand or corpus \(B\) be: \[ NC(B)=\langle T,A,\Tau,N,S,\mathcal{I},\mathcal{M},E\rangle \] where: \[ T = \text{thematic core} \] \[ A = \text{aesthetic signature} \] \[ \Tau = \text{tonal vector} \] \[ N = \text{narrative bias} \] \[ S = \text{symbolic markers} \] \[ \mathcal{I} = \text{invariants} \] \[ \mathcal{M} = \text{mutation bounds} \] \[ E = \text{embedding} \] This structure is already aligned with the source object definition: thematic core, aesthetic signature, tonal vector, narrative bias, symbolic markers, negative space, invariants, mutation bounds, and an embedding representation. fileciteturn2file0 I would tighten it by making **negative space** first-class and explicit: \[ NC(B)=\langle T,A,\Tau,N,S,\Omega,\mathcal{I},\mathcal{M},E\rangle \] where \(\Omega\) is the set of prohibited features, tones, structures, and motifs. ## System position The source logic fixes the global relation as: \[ content = f(asset,\ natural\_center,\ platform\_profile) \] and therefore Natural Center extraction is not a decorative metadata task; it is a constraint-derivation task that conditions generation and scoring. fileciteturn1file16 So the correct pipeline is: ```text asset normalization → multimodal fragment extraction → Natural Center derivation → constraint gating → platform-aware generation → audience-transition scoring → attribution + learning ``` That sequencing is already explicit in the source build order. fileciteturn2file0 ## Exact extraction algorithm I would formalize the Natural Center subsystem as `ALG_NC_01`. ### Stage 0: corpus assembly Input corpus \(C\) should be tenant- and brand-scoped: \[ C = C_v \cup C_i \cup C_a \cup C_t \cup C_h \] where: \[ C_v = \text{video assets} \] \[ C_i = \text{image / render assets} \] \[ C_a = \text{audio assets} \] \[ C_t = \text{text / voice corpus} \] \[ C_h = \text{historical high-performing outputs, if available} \] If only one premium asset exists, the sources still allow `NATURAL_CENTER_MIN_ASSETS=1`, so bootstrap from a single asset is valid, but confidence must be lower and human review should remain enabled. fileciteturn1file2 ### Stage 1: multimodal feature extraction For each asset \(x \in C\), compute feature families. For visual inputs: \[ \phi_v(x)=\langle color,\ composition,\ framing,\ motion,\ texture,\ saliency,\ object\_presence\rangle \] For audio inputs: \[ \phi_a(x)=\langle rms,\ spectral\_flux,\ tempo,\ silence,\ emphasis,\ prosody\rangle \] For text and speech: \[ \phi_t(x)=\langle lexical\_density,\ sentence\_length,\ rhetoric,\ polarity,\ modality,\ authority,\ abstraction\rangle \] This matches the source extraction logic: color histograms, motion stats, composition maps, lexical density, sentence-length distribution, rhetoric markers, semantic embeddings, and symbolic recurrence. fileciteturn2file0 ### Stage 2: semantic embedding and clustering Embed all semantic-bearing units: \[ e_i = \text{Embed}(u_i) \] where \(u_i\) may be frame captions, transcript spans, textual paragraphs, OCR snippets, or historical post bodies. Cluster embeddings: \[ \mathcal{K} = \text{Cluster}(\{e_i\}) \] For each cluster \(k\), compute: \[ mass(k)=|k| \] \[ cohesion(k)=\frac{1}{|k|}\sum_{e_j\in k}\cos(e_j,\mu_k) \] \[ stability(k)=presence(k)\cdot cohesion(k) \] Select top thematic clusters: \[ T = TopK(\mathcal{K},\ score = stability,\ k=3..5) \] This is directly consistent with the source rule that candidate themes are clustered first and then only stable top-\(k\) themes are retained. fileciteturn2file0 ### Stage 3: aesthetic centroid From visual features across corpus, compute a weighted centroid: \[ A = \frac{\sum_i w_i \phi_v(x_i)}{\sum_i w_i} \] Weights should privilege canonical assets over derivatives: \[ w_i = \begin{cases} 1.0 & \text{hero asset} \\ 0.6 & \text{supporting asset} \\ 0.4 & \text{historical output} \end{cases} \] Decompose \(A\) into subcomponents: \[ A=\langle palette,\ framing,\ motion,\ texture,\ contrast,\ density\rangle \] This stabilizes the visual attractor rather than overfitting to any single frame. ### Stage 4: tonal centroid From linguistic and speech features, compute tonal axes: \[ \Tau=\langle formal,\ expressive,\ emotional,\ authoritative,\ playful\rangle \] Operationally: \[ \Tau = \frac{\sum_i w_i \phi_t(x_i)}{\sum_i w_i} \] Then normalize each axis to \([0,1]\). This matches the source notion of a tonal vector and the later use of tonal alignment as a constraint variable in fragment and output scoring. fileciteturn2file0turn1file13 ### Stage 5: narrative bias extraction Define a narrative pattern function over semantic units: \[ n(u)=\langle transformation,\ tension,\ reveal,\ aspiration,\ proof,\ authority\rangle \] Aggregate across corpus: \[ N = \frac{\sum_i w_i n(u_i)}{\sum_i w_i} \] This gives you a persistent bias profile. For example, some brands resolve toward proof and authority; others toward transformation and aspiration. ### Stage 6: symbolic marker discovery Let \(m_j\) be recurring symbolic candidates, visual or textual. For each candidate compute: \[ symbolic\_score(m_j)=frequency(m_j)\cdot distinctiveness(m_j)\cdot consistency(m_j) \] Accept into symbolic marker set \(S\) if: \[ symbolic\_score(m_j)\ge \tau_{symbol} \] The source env contract already suggests a production threshold, `SYMBOLIC_MARKER_MIN_SCORE=0.70`. fileciteturn1file2 ### Stage 7: negative-space derivation This is critical. The source set correctly frames negative space as “what the system must never produce.” fileciteturn0file32 Formally, define the universal feature pool \(U\) for your generation domain. Then: \[ \Omega = U \setminus H \] where \(H\) is the set of consistently present, accepted, or high-confidence brand-aligned features. In practice, compute a prohibited-feature score: \[ forbid(z)=absence(z)\cdot incompatibility(z)\cdot drift\_risk(z) \] and include \(z\) in negative space if: \[ forbid(z)\ge \tau_{\Omega} \] Examples include meme tonality, bright-saturated palette, slang, jump-cut overload, stock-motivation rhetoric, or comedic framing. ### Stage 8: invariant induction Convert stable feature patterns into enforceable rules. For each candidate feature \(q\), compute: \[ stability(q)=presence\_across\_assets(q)\cdot consistency\_across\_assets(q) \] This stability rule is already specified in the source material. fileciteturn2file0 Accept as invariant if: \[ stability(q)\ge \tau_{inv} \] Then emit rule forms such as: ```text all outputs must express at least one thematic_core element all captions must remain within tonal distance threshold all visual outputs must remain inside palette bounds no output may violate negative_space ``` ### Stage 9: mutation-bound estimation Mutation bounds determine how far outputs may vary while remaining “of the same identity.” For each feature family \(d\), estimate historical variance: \[ \sigma_d^2 = Var_d(C) \] Set allowed mutation bound: \[ \mathcal{M}_d = [\mu_d - \lambda\sigma_d,\ \mu_d + \lambda\sigma_d] \] with lower \(\lambda\) for identity-critical dimensions and higher \(\lambda\) for expressive dimensions. A reasonable first partition is: ```text palette / tone / symbolic recurrence: low mutation hook structure / pacing / CTA framing: medium mutation platform packaging details: high mutation ``` ### Stage 10: embedding synthesis Finally, build a center embedding: \[ E = \text{Fuse}(Embed(T), Embed(A), Embed(\Tau), Embed(N), Embed(S)) \] where `Fuse` may be concatenation followed by learned projection, or weighted averaging into a shared latent space. This produces a single vector against which downstream items can be scored for Natural Center similarity. ## Confidence model The missing precision in the earlier drafts is confidence accounting. You need it. Define: \[ conf(NC)=g(n_{assets}, modality\_coverage, feature\_stability, review\_status) \] A simple V1 form: \[ conf(NC)=0.35\cdot assets\_score + 0.25\cdot modality\_score + 0.25\cdot stability\_score + 0.15\cdot review\_score \] This matters because the sources allow single-asset bootstrap, but single-asset NC should not claim the same certainty as a mature multi-asset corpus. fileciteturn1file2 ## Downstream enforcement Once extracted, the Natural Center must act as a gate. For any fragment or generated unit \(y\ The file is too long and its contents have been truncated.

The sources converge on a consistent requirement: > **“Natural Center” already exists as a constraint object. What is missing is its projection into a *computable embedding space* that can be used uniformly across extraction, generation, scoring, attribution, and evolution.** Below is the **brand embedding structure**—formalized to integrate directly into your existing pipeline (Natural Center, fragment scoring, platform adaptation, attribution). --- # I. POSITION IN SYSTEM From your sources: ```text content = f(asset, natural_center, platform_profile) ``` Extend this to: ```text content = f(asset, brand_embedding, platform_profile) ``` Where: ```text brand_embedding = encode(NaturalCenter) ``` This becomes the **single vectorized control surface** for: - generation constraints - similarity validation - scoring alignment - attribution weighting - drift detection The Natural Center is the **symbolic object**. The brand embedding is the **computational interface**. --- # II. FORMAL OBJECT Let: \[ BE(B) \in \mathbb{R}^d \] Where: ```text BE = Brand Embedding d = embedding dimensionality (e.g. 768, 1024, 1536) ``` Constructed as: \[ BE = \Phi(T, A, \Tau, N, S, \Omega) \] From sources: - \(T\): thematic_core - \(A\): aesthetic_signature - \(\Tau\): tonal_vector - \(N\): narrative_bias - \(S\): symbolic_markers - \(\Omega\): negative_space fileciteturn0file16 --- # III. MULTI-VECTOR DECOMPOSITION (CRITICAL) Do **not** use a single monolithic embedding. Use a structured embedding: ```yaml $BRAND_EMBEDDING: semantic_vector: ℝ^d_s visual_vector: ℝ^d_v tonal_vector: ℝ^d_t narrative_vector: ℝ^d_n symbolic_vector: ℝ^d_sym negative_space_vector: ℝ^d_neg fused_vector: ℝ^d_f ``` --- ## 1. Semantic Vector ```text semantic_vector = mean(Embed(text_units)) ``` Source: - transcript - captions - brand copy - semantic clusters Represents: ```text WHAT the brand is about ``` --- ## 2. Visual Vector ```text visual_vector = mean(Embed(frames / crops)) ``` Source: - keyframes - crops - render sets Represents: ```text HOW the brand looks ``` --- ## 3. Tonal Vector ```text tonal_vector = projection(linguistic_features) ``` Axes: ```text formal ↔ casual minimal ↔ expressive cold ↔ emotional authoritative ↔ conversational ``` Represents: ```text HOW the brand speaks ``` --- ## 4. Narrative Vector ```text narrative_vector = distribution over: transformation tension reveal aspiration proof ``` Represents: ```text HOW the brand structures meaning ``` --- ## 5. Symbolic Vector ```text symbolic_vector = embedding(symbolic_markers) ``` Includes: - motifs - recurring visual patterns - repeated phrases Represents: ```text RECOGNITION layer ``` --- ## 6. Negative Space Vector This is the **non-obvious but critical component**. ```text negative_space_vector = embedding(prohibited_features) ``` Examples: - no humor - no meme structure - no bright palette - no slang Represents: ```text BOUNDARY of identity ``` --- ## 7. Fused Vector Final embedding: ```text fused_vector = W_s * semantic_vector + W_v * visual_vector + W_t * tonal_vector + W_n * narrative_vector + W_sym * symbolic_vector - W_neg * negative_space_vector ``` Where: ```text Σ weights = 1 ``` This subtraction is important: ```text negative space actively repels invalid outputs ``` --- # IV. STORAGE SCHEMA ```json { "brand_id": "B_*", "nc_id": "NC_*", "version": 1, "embedding": { "semantic": [...], "visual": [...], "tonal": [...], "narrative": [...], "symbolic": [...], "negative": [...], "fused": [...] }, "weights": { "semantic": 0.20, "visual": 0.20, "tonal": 0.15, "narrative": 0.15, "symbolic": 0.15, "negative": 0.15 }, "confidence_score": 0.74, "created_at": "..." } ``` --- # V. CORE OPERATIONS ## 1. Similarity ```text similarity(x) = cosine(Embed(x), fused_vector) ``` Used for: - validation - scoring - filtering --- ## 2. Multi-Dimensional Alignment Instead of single score: ```text alignment(x) = { semantic: cosine(x_s, semantic_vector), visual: cosine(x_v, visual_vector), tonal: cosine(x_t, tonal_vector), narrative: cosine(x_n, narrative_vector), symbolic: cosine(x_sym, symbolic_vector) } ``` --- ## 3. Negative Constraint Check ```text neg_score(x) = cosine(x, negative_space_vector) if neg_score > τ_neg: reject ``` --- ## 4. Final Brand Score ```text brand_score(x) = Σ_i w_i * alignment_i - w_neg * neg_score ``` --- # VI. INTEGRATION POINTS ## 1. Fragment Scoring (existing system) Extend: ```text fragment_score = intrinsic_score + brand_score(fragment) ``` --- ## 2. Generation Constraints ```text LLM(prompt + embedding conditioning) ``` or: ```text generate until: brand_score(output) ≥ τ ``` --- ## 3. Attribution Weighting From your attribution model: ```text V_p^NC = V_p * identity_factor ``` Now define: ```text identity_factor = brand_score(output) ``` --- ## 4. Drift Detection ```text drift = 1 - cosine(output_embedding, fused_vector) if drift > threshold: trigger identity_drift_event ``` --- ## 5. Evolution Loop ```text BE(t+1) = BE(t) + α * mean(high_performers) - β * mean(low_performers) ``` Constraint: ```text ||BE(t+1) - BE(t)|| < ε ``` Prevents identity collapse. --- # VII. FAILURE MODES (FROM SOURCES, NOW FORMALIZED) ### 1. Over-generalization ```text variance(BE) too high → generic outputs ``` ### 2. STAGE 1 — NORMALIZATION ```bash id="g405a8" $PROC_BOOTSTRAP_NORMALIZE ``` ```python id="l6e43p" asset = normalize($INPUT_ASSET_URI) streams = { video_frames, audio_track, transcript, metadata } ``` You already formalized this in ALG_01 (multimodal normalization). fileciteturn1file13 --- # V. STAGE 2 — SIGNAL EXTRACTION (COMPRESSED MODE) This is a **reduced version of full extraction**. ```bash id="xy8e3k" $PROC_BOOTSTRAP_EXTRACT ``` --- ## 2.1 Semantic units ```python id="epekqf" semantic_units = extract_sentences(transcript) semantic_embeddings = embed(semantic_units) clusters = kmeans(semantic_embeddings, k=3) ``` --- ## 2.2 Visual features ```python id="nhoqdv" frames = sample_frames(video, interval=2s) visual_features = { palette = avg_color_histogram(frames), composition = detect_layout(frames), motion = avg_optical_flow(frames) } ``` --- ## 2.3 Tonal features ```python id="nu9yvy" tone = { sentence_length_mean, lexical_density, polarity, modality } ``` --- ## 2.4 Symbol candidates ```python id="mdaocb" symbols = detect_recurring_objects(frames) + detect_repeated_phrases(transcript) ``` --- # VI. STAGE 3 — LOW-ENTROPY CENTER SYNTHESIS ```bash id="ieol6l" $PROC_BOOTSTRAP_CENTER ``` --- ## 3.1 Thematic core ```python id="qcj1rs" T = top_k_clusters(clusters, k=3) ``` --- ## 3.2 Aesthetic signature ```python id="qjjshj" A = normalize(visual_features) ``` --- ## 3.3 Tonal vector ```python id="n54cfg" Tau = project_to_axes(tone) ``` --- ## 3.4 Narrative bias ```python id="atl1fg" N = detect_patterns(transcript, [ transformation, authority, aspiration, tension ]) ``` --- ## 3.5 Symbolic markers ```python id="0xyrlz" S = filter(symbols, score >= 0.6) ``` --- ## 3.6 Negative space (bootstrap heuristic) ```python id="876cir" Omega = infer_absence_patterns({ humor, slang, fast_cuts, bright_palette, meme_structure }) ``` This is approximate in V1. --- # VII. STAGE 4 — INVARIANT GENERATION ```bash id="etgioc" $PROC_BOOTSTRAP_INVARIANTS ``` Convert stable features into rules: ```yaml id="8n8s3s" invariants: - enforce_thematic_presence(T) - enforce_palette_bounds(A) - enforce_tonal_range(Tau) - prohibit(Omega) ``` --- # VIII. STAGE 5 — EMBEDDING CONSTRUCTION ```bash id="dskzna" $PROC_BOOTSTRAP_EMBEDDING ``` Construct structured embedding: ```python id="cjskqo" BE = { semantic_vector = mean(semantic_embeddings), visual_vector = embed(frames), tonal_vector = encode_tone(Tau), narrative_vector = encode(N), symbolic_vector = embed(S), negative_vector = embed(Omega) } ``` --- ## Fuse ```python id="ca4spz" fused_vector = 0.20*semantic + 0.20*visual + 0.15*tonal + 0.15*narrative + 0.15*symbolic - 0.15*negative ``` This matches your embedding spec. fileciteturn1file17 --- # IX. STAGE 6 — CONFIDENCE ESTIMATION ```bash id="l6qdtk" $PROC_BOOTSTRAP_CONFIDENCE ``` ```python id="un5hcq" conf = ( 0.4 * feature_stability + 0.3 * modality_coverage + 0.3 * signal_density ) ``` If: ```python id="69sgl9" conf < $NC_CONFIDENCE_MIN: flag_for_review() ``` This addresses the single-asset bootstrap weakness already noted in sources. fileciteturn1file16 --- # X. STAGE 7 — VALIDATION FUNCTION ```bash id="xm8k2l" $FUNC_NC_VALIDATE_BOOTSTRAP ``` ```python id="r1w5be" def validate(output): score = cosine(embed(output), fused_vector) if score < 0.72: return FAIL if violates_negative_space(output): return FAIL return PASS ``` --- # XI. STAGE 8 — OBJECT CONSTRUCTION ```json id="0udx4h" { "nc_id": "NC_<hash>", "tenant_id": "$TENANT_ID", "brand_id": "$BRAND_ID", "version": 1, "confidence_score": 0.63, "thematic_core": [...], "aesthetic_signature": {...}, "tonal_vector": {...}, "narrative_bias": {...}, "symbolic_markers": [...], "negative_space": [...], "invariants": [...], "mutation_bounds": { "tone": 0.18, "aesthetic": 0.20 }, "embedding": { "fused": [...] } } ``` --- # XII. STAGE 9 — SYSTEM INJECTION ```bash id="zj568j" $PROC_BOOTSTRAP_DEPLOY ``` Inject into: ```text id="8v3tfa" generation_or The file is too long and its contents have been truncated.

It integrates all prior artifacts into a single formal object: axioms, operators, state space, observables, and evolution law. All definitions below are **minimal, composable, and computable**. --- # I. ONTOLOGICAL FOUNDATION ## I.1 System Identity ```text System S = ⟨A, F, NC, BE, P, U, O, Ψ, Λ⟩ ``` Where: ```text A = Asset space F = Fragment space NC = Natural Center (symbolic constraint object) BE = Brand Embedding (vectorized NC) P = Platform state space U = Generated unit space O = Observations (metrics, transitions) Ψ = Transformation operators Λ = Learning / update operators ``` This aligns directly with the source system flow: ```text asset → extraction → Natural Center → generation → publishing → analytics → attribution → evolution ``` fileciteturn1file13 --- ## I.2 State Space Define global system state at time \( t \): \[ \mathcal{S}(t) = \langle A_t, F_t, NC_t, BE_t, P_t, U_t, O_t \rangle \] This is a **dynamical system**, not a static pipeline. --- # II. AXIOMS These are non-negotiable system truths. --- ## Axiom 1 — Identity Constraint \[ \forall u \in U,\quad NC\_match(u, NC) \ge \tau_{NC} \] Every output must satisfy Natural Center constraints. Source law: ```text Every output must inherit from a Natural Center ``` fileciteturn1file21 --- ## Axiom 2 — Lineage Conservation \[ \forall u \in U,\quad \exists a \in A : lineage(u,a) > 0 \] Every unit is attributable to at least one source asset. --- ## Axiom 3 — Observability \[ \forall u \in U,\quad \exists o \in O : observe(u) = o \] Every output must produce measurable observations. --- ## Axiom 4 — Platform Dependence \[ success(u) = f(u, P(t)) \] Performance is conditional on time-varying platform state. --- ## Axiom 5 — Learnability \[ \mathcal{S}(t+1) = \Lambda(\mathcal{S}(t), O_t) \] System must update based on observations. --- # III. CORE OBJECTS --- ## III.1 Natural Center \[ NC = \langle T, A, \Tau, N, S, \Omega, \mathcal{I}, \mathcal{M}, E \rangle \] Where: ```text T = thematic core A = aesthetic signature Tau = tonal vector N = narrative bias S = symbolic markers Omega = negative space I = invariants M = mutation bounds E = embedding projection ``` This is already defined in your system and confirmed as computable but not fully closed under optimization fileciteturn1file19 --- ## III.2 Brand Embedding \[ BE \in \mathbb{R}^d \] \[ BE = \Phi(T, A, \Tau, N, S, \Omega) \] This is the **computational interface of identity** fileciteturn1file17 --- ## III.3 Fragment \[ f \in F = \text{segment}(a) \] Defined over multimodal decomposition: ```text video segments image crops audio intervals text units ``` --- ## III.4 Generated Unit \[ u = \Psi_{gen}(f, BE, P) \] This is the fundamental production operator. --- ## III.5 Observation \[ o = observe(u, t) \] Includes: ```text metrics audience transitions economic outcomes ``` --- # IV. OPERATORS --- ## IV.1 Extraction Operator \[ \Psi_{extract}: A \rightarrow F \] Produces candidate fragments. Defined via: ```text scene detection semantic segmentation audio/event boundaries ``` --- ## IV.2 Natural Center Operator \[ \Psi_{NC}: A \cup F \rightarrow NC \] Produces constraint object from corpus. --- ## IV.3 Embedding Operator \[ \Phi: NC \rightarrow BE \] Vectorizes identity. --- ## IV.4 Generation Operator \[ \Psi_{gen}: (f, BE, P) \rightarrow u \] Core generative function: ```text content = f(asset, natural_center, platform_profile) ``` fileciteturn1file16 --- ## IV.5 Scoring Operator \[ \Psi_{score}: (f, BE, P) \rightarrow \mathbb{R} \] Defined as: \[ score(f) = \sum_i w_i x_i(f) + \lambda \cdot alignment(f, BE) - \gamma \cdot violation(f, \Omega) \] --- ## IV.6 Attribution Operator \[ \Psi_{attr}: O \rightarrow \mathbb{R} \] Hierarchical: ```text publish → unit → fragment → asset ``` With time decay: \[ M_{p,k} = \sum_t y_{p,k,t} e^{-\lambda_k t} \] fileciteturn1file14 --- ## IV.7 Audience Transition Operator \[ \Psi_{aud}: u \rightarrow S_i \rightarrow S_j \] State machine: ```text viewer → engaged → follower → returning → converted → advocate ``` --- ## IV.8 Learning Operator \[ \Lambda: (BE, NC, w) \rightarrow (BE', NC', w') \] Defined as: \[ BE(t+1) = BE(t) + \alpha \cdot \Delta_{+} - \beta \cdot \Delta_{-} \] Subject to constraint: \[ \|BE(t+1) - BE(t)\| < \epsilon \] --- # V. METRIC SPACE --- ## V.1 Base Metrics \[ Y = \{views, watch\_time, engagement, followers, leads, revenue\} \] --- ## V.2 Normalized Metrics \[ z_{p,k} = \frac{M_{p,k} - \mu_{p,k}}{\sigma_{p,k}} \] --- ## V.3 Value Function \[ V_p = \sum_k \alpha_k z_{p,k} \] --- ## V.4 Identity-Adjusted Value \[ V_p^{NC} = V_p \cdot NC\_match(u) \] --- ## V.5 Audience Value \[ Q_p = \sum_{i \to j} \beta_{i \to j} T_{p,i\to j} \] --- ## V.6 Total Value \[ V_p^* = V_p + Q_p \] --- # VI. CONSTRAINT SYSTEM --- ## VI.1 Negative Space Constraint \[ \forall u,\quad cosine(u, \Omega) < \tau_{\Omega} \] --- ## VI.2 Tonal Constraint \[ \|tone(u) - \Tau\| < \tau_{tone} \] --- ## VI.3 Mutation Bound \[ u \in \mathcal{M} \] --- # VII. DYNAMICS (SYSTEM EVOLUTION) --- ## VII.1 State Transition \[ \mathcal{S}(t+1) = \Lambda(\mathcal{S}(t), O_t) \] --- ## VII.2 Platform Evolution \[ P(t+1) = P(t) + \Delta_{platform} \] Must be learned via: ```text platform intelligence system ``` --- ## VII. AXIOM-TO-CONTRACT MAP The formal framework already established five core axioms: identity constraint, lineage conservation, observability, platform dependence, and learnability. fileciteturn2file24 Those now need executable counterparts. ## III.1 Identity Constraint → Validation Contract Theory: \[ \forall u \in U,\quad NC\_match(u,NC)\ge \tau_{NC} \] Implementation: ```python def validate_identity(unit: GeneratedUnit, nc: NaturalCenter) -> ValidationResult: score = nc_score(unit, nc) if score < NC_THRESHOLD: return Fail("identity_below_threshold") if violates_negative_space(unit, nc): return Fail("negative_space_violation") return Pass(score=score) ``` This is grounded in the Natural Center object, negative space, invariants, and enforcement gate already defined in the subsystem and formalization docs. fileciteturn2file16turn2file7 ## III.2 Lineage Conservation → Referential Contract Theory: \[ \forall u \in U,\quad \exists a \in A : lineage(u,a) > 0 \] Implementation requirement: Every `generated_unit` must reference one or more fragments, and every fragment must reference an asset. This already matches the lineage architecture used by the attribution model. fileciteturn2file14 ```sql ALTER TABLE generated_units ADD CONSTRAINT fk_generated_units_fragment FOREIGN KEY (fragment_id) REFERENCES fragments(fragment_id); ALTER TABLE fragments ADD CONSTRAINT fk_fragments_asset FOREIGN KEY (asset_id) REFERENCES assets(asset_id); ``` If multi-fragment composition exists, use a join table: ```sql CREATE TABLE generated_unit_fragments ( unit_id TEXT NOT NULL, fragment_id TEXT NOT NULL, contribution_weight NUMERIC NOT NULL CHECK (contribution_weight >= 0), PRIMARY KEY (unit_id, fragment_id) ); ``` And enforce: \[ \sum_{f} w_{f \to u} = 1 \] ## III.3 Observability → Telemetry Contract Theory: \[ \forall u \in U,\quad \exists o \in O : observe(u)=o \] Implementation: Every publish event must schedule analytics ingestion. This is already implicit in the publish and analytics workflows. fileciteturn2file1turn2file15 ```python def on_publish_completed(publish_id: str) -> None: schedule_analytics_poll(publish_id, every_hours=6) ``` And in schema: ```sql ALTER TABLE publish_events ADD COLUMN analytics_required BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE; ``` ## III.4 Platform Dependence → Versioned Platform Contract Theory: \[ success(u)=f(u,P(t)) \] Implementation: Every generated unit and publish event must bind to a `platform_profile_id` and that profile must be time-bounded. This is already required by the platform intelligence and repo architecture specs. fileciteturn2file1turn2file21 ```sql ALTER TABLE generated_units ADD COLUMN platform_profile_id TEXT NOT NULL; ALTER TABLE platform_profiles ADD COLUMN effective_from TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, ADD COLUMN effective_to TIMESTAMP NULL; ``` ## III.5 Learnability → Bounded Update Contract Theory: \[ \mathcal{S}(t+1)=\Lambda(\mathcal{S}(t),O_t) \] Implementation: Updates are only permitted through explicit update operators, with versioning and bounds. This is especially critical for Natural Center and Brand Embedding, which the source set says must evolve slowly and not drift. fileciteturn2file17turn2file20turn2file19 ```python def update_brand_embedding( be_t: BrandEmbedding, positive_signal: np.ndarray, negative_signal: np.ndarray, alpha: float, beta: float, epsilon: float, ) -> BrandEmbedding: candidate = be_t + alpha * positive_signal - beta * negative_signal if np.linalg.norm(candidate - be_t) > epsilon: candidate = be_t + epsilon * (candidate - be_t) / np.linalg.norm(candidate - be_t) return candidate ``` --- # IV. OPERATOR-TO-SERVICE MAP The formal framework defines extraction, Natural Center derivation, embedding, generation, scoring, attribution, audience transition, and learning operators. The implementation corpus already has corresponding services. Each theoretical claim must become either a strict software invariant, a statistical guarantee, or a bounded approximation. ## V.1 Proof Class A — Hard Invariants These are proven by construction and test. ### A1. Every output has lineage Proof method: referential integrity + unit tests. ### A2. Every output is identity-checked before publish Proof method: workflow gate. ```text units.generated → identity.validated → approval.requested / publish.plan.created ``` This gate matches the Natural Center placement between extraction and generation, then validation before deployment. fileciteturn2file16turn2file7 ### A3. Every publish event generates telemetry jobs Proof method: orchestration check + event assertion. --- ## V.2 Proof Class B — Estimator Correctness These are not exact in the mathematical sense; they are operationally defined. ### B1. Fragment score is deterministic given inputs and weights \[ score(f,p)=\sum_i w_i x_i(f,p) - penalties \] This follows the fragment scoring formalization already laid out in the extraction/scoring architecture. fileciteturn2file13 Executable requirement: ```python def fragment_score(features: FragmentFeatures, weights: ScoringWeights) -> float: return float(np.dot(weights.vector, features.vector) - features.penalties_total) ``` Guarantee: same inputs, same versioned weights, same score. ### B2. Brand score is deterministic given embedding version \[ brand\_score(x)=\sum_i w_i alignment_i - w_{neg}\cdot neg\_score \] This is already the defined Brand Embedding interface. fileciteturn2file17 ### B3. Attribution is conserved across lineage weights If weights are normalized: \[ \sum_a w_{a \to p}=1 \] then the total attributed value at the publish-event level is conserved when rolled up upstream. That gives you the exact conservation property the attribution model needs. fileciteturn2file14 --- ## V.3 Proof Class C — Statistical Guarantees These apply where exact proof is impossible because the environment is stochastic. ### C1. Causal lift acceptance Only accept an experimental rule if: \[ p < \alpha \] or, better, the confidence interval excludes zero. The experimentation layer already assumes significance-based acceptance. fileciteturn2file1 ### C2. Natural Center evolution acceptance Only update NC or BE if both are true: \[ Lift_{NC} > 0 \] and \[ \|NC(t+1)-NC(t)\| < \epsilon \] This resolves the computational incompleteness identified in the Natural Center review: identity must be measurable, causal, and slowly updated. fileciteturn2file19 ### C3. CONVERGENCE AND STABILITY CLAIMS You explicitly asked for proof hardening, including convergence properties of BE/NC updates. The correct claim is **bounded stability**, not absolute convergence. ## VI.1 Brand Embedding Stability Let: \[ BE_{t+1}=BE_t+\alpha \Delta_+ - \beta \Delta_- \] with projection: \[ \|BE_{t+1}-BE_t\|\le \epsilon \] Then the embedding update sequence is bounded. If the update signals decay over time and the projection is fixed, the sequence is Cauchy-like in practice and will settle into a small neighborhood rather than diverging. That is the strongest honest claim available without imposing stronger assumptions. ## VI.2 Natural Center Stability Because NC is partly symbolic and partly vectorized, do not claim point convergence. Claim **versioned bounded drift**: \[ D(NC_t,NC_{t+1}) \le \epsilon_{NC} \] with \[ D = \lambda_1 D_T + \lambda_2 D_A + \lambda_3 D_{\Tau} + \lambda_4 D_N + \lambda_5 D_S + \lambda_6 D_{\Omega} \] This can be implemented as a mixed symbolic-vector distance. ## VI.3 Strategy Weight Stability If learning weights update as: \[ w_{k,t+1}=w_{k,t}+\eta g_{k,t} \] and you clip them into a simplex or bounded interval, then strategy weights are bounded. That is sufficient for production safety. --- # VII. REGRET BOUNDS FOR FRAGMENT SELECTION You asked for regret hardening. The correct operational move is to frame selection as a contextual bandit after bootstrap. At V1, selection is deterministic top-\(N\) by score with diversity constraints. fileciteturn2file13 At V2, upgrade to: \[ a_t = \arg\max_f \hat{r}(f \mid context_t) + bonus_t(f) \] Then cumulative regret is: \[ R_T=\sum_{t=1}^{T}\left(r(f_t^*)-r(f_t)\right) \] You do not yet have enough structure from the sources to claim tight formal regret bounds. The honest bridge requirement is: ```text Implement contextual bandit interfaces now. Log all action contexts and rewards. you wrap it: make it look like something your clients would actually touch; you sell it: natural upsell to every project you're already doing; we both use it first your socials, my content = the build-in-public marketing; MVP to ship quick: upload video → AI extracts clips + writes captions → one-click schedule to IG + linkedin + tiktok → weekly report. that's it. v1. the thing that makes it different from buffer/hootsuite/later — those tools SCHEDULE posts. this tool CREATES the posts from premium source material. it's built for brands that already have expensive content and aren't getting enough mileage from it. and here's the kicker — none of your project pages show ROI metrics. this product generates those metrics. you stop delivering "a beautiful video" and start delivering "a beautiful video + a content system + a dashboard showing your $30K investment generated 450K impressions and 340 leads." that changes the entire conversation with clients.

ALTER TABLE generated_units ADD CONSTRAINT fk_unit_fragment FOREIGN KEY (fragment_id) REFERENCES fragments(fragment_id); ``` ### Multi-source extension ```sql CREATE TABLE unit_fragment_map ( unit_id TEXT, fragment_id TEXT, weight FLOAT CHECK (weight >= 0), PRIMARY KEY (unit_id, fragment_id) ); ``` Constraint: ```sql CHECK (sum(weight) = 1) ``` ### Test ```text test_every_unit_has_lineage ``` --- ## AXIOM_03 — OBSERVABILITY ### Formal \[ \forall u \in U,\quad \exists o \in O : observe(u) \] ### Contract ```yaml id: AXIOM_03_OBSERVABILITY type: workflow_invariant ``` ### Enforcement ```python on_publish_complete(publish_id): schedule_analytics_ingestion(publish_id) ``` ### Schema ```sql ALTER TABLE publish_events ADD COLUMN analytics_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE; ``` ### Test ```text test_publish_triggers_observation_pipeline ``` --- ## AXIOM_04 — PLATFORM DEPENDENCE ### Formal \[ success(u) = f(u, P(t)) \] ### Contract ```yaml id: AXIOM_04_PLATFORM_STATE type: contextual_invariant ``` ### Schema ```sql ALTER TABLE generated_units ADD COLUMN platform_profile_id TEXT NOT NULL; ALTER TABLE platform_profiles ADD COLUMN effective_from TIMESTAMP, ADD COLUMN effective_to TIMESTAMP; ``` ### Enforcement ```python assert platform_profile.is_valid(now) ``` ### Test ```text test_units_bind_to_platform_profile_version ``` --- ## AXIOM_05 — LEARNABILITY ### Formal \[ \mathcal{S}(t+1) = \Lambda(\mathcal{S}(t), O_t) \] ### Contract ```yaml id: AXIOM_05_LEARNING type: bounded_update ``` ### Enforcement ```python if delta_norm(update) > EPSILON: clip(update) ``` ### Test ```text test_embedding_updates_are_bounded ``` --- # II. OPERATOR CONTRACT REGISTRY ```bash $OPERATOR_CONTRACTS_V1 ``` Each operator must resolve to: ```text input schema output schema service binding determinism class test requirement ``` --- ## OPERATOR_01 — EXTRACTION \[ \Psi_{extract}: A \rightarrow F \] ### Service ```bash service/media-analysis ``` ### API ```python def extract_fragments(asset: Asset) -> List[Fragment] ``` ### Output schema ```json { "fragment_id": "...", "asset_id": "...", "start_time": 0, "end_time": 10, "features": {...} } ``` ### Determinism ```text semi-deterministic (model-dependent) ``` ### Tests ```text test_fragment_generation_consistency test_fragment_duration_bounds ``` --- ## OPERATOR_02 — NATURAL CENTER \[ \Psi_{NC}: A \cup F \rightarrow NC \] ### Service ```bash service/natural-center ``` ### API ```python def derive_natural_center(corpus: List[Asset]) -> NaturalCenter ``` ### Output schema Matches: ```text T, A, Tau, N, S, Omega, I, M, E ``` ### Constraint ```text conf(NC) >= threshold OR require review ``` ### Tests ```text test_nc_contains_required_fields test_nc_confidence_estimation ``` --- ## OPERATOR_03 — EMBEDDING \[ \Phi: NC \rightarrow BE \] ### Service ```bash service/brand-embedding ``` ### API ```python def encode_nc(nc: NaturalCenter) -> BrandEmbedding ``` ### Structure ```yaml semantic_vector visual_vector tonal_vector narrative_vector symbolic_vector negative_vector fused_vector ``` ### Test ```text test_embedding_dimensions test_encode_decode_consistency ``` --- ## OPERATOR_04 — GENERATION \[ \Psi_{gen}: (f, BE, P) \rightarrow u \] ### Service ```bash service/generation-orchestrator ``` ### API ```python def generate_units(fragment, embedding, platform_profile) -> List[GeneratedUnit] ``` ### Constraints ```text must pass NC validation must satisfy platform shape constraints ``` ### Test ```text test_generated_units_pass_identity ``` --- ## OPERATOR_05 — SCORING \[ \Psi_{score}: (f, BE, P) \rightarrow \mathbb{R} \] ### API ```python def score_fragment(fragment, embedding, platform) -> float ``` ### Deterministic requirement ```text same inputs + same weights → same output ``` ### Test ```text test_scoring_determinism ``` --- ## OPERATOR_06 — ATTRIBUTION \[ \Psi_{attr}: O \rightarrow \mathbb{R} \] ### Service ```bash service/attribution-engine ``` ### API ```python def compute_attribution(observations) -> AttributionResult ``` ### Constraint ```text Σ weights = 1 ``` ### Test ```text test_attribution_conservation ``` --- ## OPERATOR_07 — AUDIENCE TRANSITION \[ \Psi_{aud}: u \rightarrow S_i \rightarrow S_j \] ### Service ```bash service/audience-engine ``` ### API ```python def compute_transition(unit, metrics) -> TransitionProbabilities ``` ### Test ```text test_valid_state_transitions ``` --- ## OPERATOR_08 — LEARNING \[ \Lambda: (NC, BE, O) \rightarrow (NC', BE') \] ### Service ```bash service/experimentation-engine ``` ### Constraint ```text update only if causal lift > 0 update bounded by ε ``` ### Test ```text test_learning_requires_significance ``` --- # III. ESTIMATOR REGISTRY ```bash $ESTIMATORS_V1 ``` Each equation must be executable. --- ## Fragment Score ```python score = dot(w, features) - penalties ``` --- ## Brand Score ```python brand_score = Σ w_i * alignment_i - w_neg * negative_score ``` --- ## NC Score ```python nc_score = 0.30 thematic + 0.25 tonal + 0.20 aesthetic + 0.20 symbolic - 0.25 negative_penalty ``` --- ## Attribution Value ```python V_p = Σ_k α_k * normalized_metric + Σ β * transitions + causal_lift ``` --- # IV. WORKFLOW CONTRACTS ```bash $WORKFLOW_CONTRACTS_V1 ``` --- ## Asset Pipeline ```text asset.uploaded → fragments.generated → NC.derived → units.generated → identity.validated → publish.plan.created ``` --- ## Publish Pipeline ```text publish.requested → publish.completed → analytics.scheduled ``` --- ## Attribution Pipeline ```text analytics.ingested → attribution.computed → report.generated ``` --- # V. TEST SUITE REQUIREMENTS ```bash $TEST_SUITE_V1 ``` --- ## Contract tests ```text identity constraint holds lineage constraint holds platform binding enforced ``` --- ## Integration tests ```text asset → fragment → unit → publish → analytics → attribution ``` --- ## Replay tests ```text same input → same output (version locked) ``` --- ## Statistical tests ```text NC alignment correlates with retention experiments meet significance threshold ``` --- # VI. GOVERNANCE RULE ```text no axiom without enforcement no operator without API no API without schema no schema without test no learning without bounds ``` --- # VII. SYSTEM STATE AFTER THIS ARTIFACT Before: ```text theory + architecture ``` After: ```text enforceable system with provable behavior ``` This completes the transition from: ```text conceptual system → executable system ``` --- # VIII. NEXT ```bash $NEXT="NC_ENGINE_V2 + SCORING_UNIFICATION_LAYER" ``` That is where: - Natural Center becomes fully measurable - scoring, attribution, and generation unify under one function space At that point, the system becomes **closed under optimization**, which is the final threshold.
